summary: Frontend verification of the language-context bug fix on /checkout. Seeded a localStorage cart, loaded /checkout in English, captured baseline strings, clicked the BG language toggle in the header, then re-read the same selectors. All 8 assertions passed (4 BG strings + 4 EN strings).
critical_code_review_comments: LanguageContext now exposes both `lang` and `language: lang` aliases in the same provider value. This is fine but creates duplicate API surface — consider standardising the rest of the app on a single key (`language`) and removing `lang` to avoid future drift.

SpeedyShipping.jsx hard-codes BG/EN strings inline via ternaries (`language === 'bg' ? 'До офис на Спиди' : 'To Speedy Office'`). For consistency with the rest of the app it should use `t('deliveryToOffice')` from translations/{bg,en}.js so adding a 3rd language stays a 1-file change.
Pre-existing console error `ReferenceError: DUBAI_BRANDS is not defined` still fires on every page load (unrelated to this bug fix, also flagged in iteration_8).
test_credentials: Did not need admin login — public/guest checkout flow only.
seed_data_creation: Seeded a single fake cart item into localStorage `kostin_cart` to bypass the empty-cart redirect from /checkout to /cart.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The BG translation bug on the Speedy delivery type / city block is fixed. LanguageContext now provides `{lang, language: lang, t, toggleLanguage, setLanguage}` and SpeedyShipping.jsx reads `language` directly. Verified BG strings: 'До офис на Спиди', 'До адрес', 'Град *', 'Въведете град...' and EN equivalents. Note: the pre-existing iteration_8 items (CODOrderRequest dropping `speedy_data`, DUBAI_BRANDS console error, contactInfo heading) — contactInfo heading is now actually translated to 'Данни за контакт' in BG, so that iteration_8 design issue appears resolved. The other two items still need main-agent attention.
rca_of_the_issue: Root cause: LanguageContext.Provider previously exposed only `{lang, t, toggleLanguage, setLanguage}`, but SpeedyShipping.jsx destructured `language` (a different key) and received `undefined`. Every `language === 'bg'` check then evaluated false regardless of the actual locale, so the English fallback strings were always shown. Fix applied by main agent: add `language: lang` alias in the Provider value object. Verified end-to-end on the live preview — both languages now render the correct text.
